speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Stand by my side, you whom the gods have made

text: Preservers of my throne. Woe is my heart

text: Stepped before targes of proof, cannot be found.

text: He shall be happy that can find him, if

text: I never saw

text: Sir,

text: Wilt thou undo the worth thou art unpaid for

text: By tasting of our wrath? How of descent

text: In that he spake too far.

text: And thou shalt die for ’t.

text: We will die all three

text: But I will prove that two on ’s are as good

text: As I have given out him.—My sons, I must

speaker: BELARIUS
text: For mine own part unfold a dangerous speech,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Though haply well for you.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Your danger’s ours.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And our good his.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Have at it, then.—By leave,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou hadst, great king, a subject who

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Was called Belarius.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: What of him? He is

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A banished traitor.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: He it is that hath

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Assumed this age; indeed a banished man,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I know not how a traitor.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Take him hence.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The whole world shall not save him.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Not too hot.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: First pay me for the nursing of thy sons

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And let it be confiscate all, so soon

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As I have received it.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Nursing of my sons?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I am too blunt and saucy. Here’s my knee.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Ere I arise I will prefer my sons,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Then spare not the old father. Mighty sir,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: These two young gentlemen that call me father

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And think they are my sons are none of mine.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: They are the issue of your loins, my liege,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And blood of your begetting.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: How? My issue?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: So sure as you your father’s. I, old Morgan,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Am that Belarius whom you sometime banished.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Your pleasure was my mere offense, my punishment

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Itself, and all my treason. That I suffered

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Was all the harm I did. These gentle princes—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: For such and so they are—these twenty years

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Have I trained up; those arts they have as I

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Could put into them. My breeding was, sir, as

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Your Highness knows. Their nurse Euriphile,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Whom for the theft I wedded, stole these children

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Upon my banishment. I moved her to ’t,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Having received the punishment before

speaker: BELARIUS
text: For that which I did then. Beaten for loyalty

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Excited me to treason. Their dear loss,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The more of you ’twas felt, the more it shaped

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Unto my end of stealing them. But, gracious sir,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Here are your sons again, and I must lose

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Two of the sweet’st companions in the world.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The benediction of these covering heavens

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Fall on their heads like dew, for they are worthy

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To inlay heaven with stars.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Thou weep’st and speak’st.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The service that you three have done is more

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Unlike than this thou tell’st. I lost my children.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: If these be they, I know not how to wish

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A pair of worthier sons.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Be pleased awhile.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This gentleman whom I call Polydor,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Most worthy prince, as yours is true Guiderius;

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This gentleman, my Cadwal, Arviragus,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Your younger princely son. He, sir, was lapped

speaker: BELARIUS
text: In a most curious mantle, wrought by th’ hand

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of his queen mother, which for more probation

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I can with ease produce.

text: Guiderius had

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Upon his neck a mole, a sanguine star.

text: It was a mark of wonder.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This is he,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Who hath upon him still that natural stamp.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: It was wise Nature’s end in the donation

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To be his evidence now.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: O, what am I,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A mother to the birth of three? Ne’er mother

text: Rejoiced deliverance more.—Blest pray you be,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: That after this strange starting from your orbs,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: You may reign in them now.—O Imogen,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Thou hast lost by this a kingdom!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: No, my lord.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I have got two worlds by ’t.—O my gentle brothers,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Have we thus met? O, never say hereafter

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But I am truest speaker. You called me “brother”

speaker: IMOGEN
text: When I was but your sister; I you “brothers”

speaker: IMOGEN
text: When we were so indeed.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Did you e’er meet?

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And at first meeting loved,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Continued so until we thought he died.

speaker: CORNELIUS
text: By the Queen’s dram she swallowed.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: O, rare instinct!

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: When shall I hear all through? This fierce abridgment

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Hath to it circumstantial branches which

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Distinction should be rich in. Where, how lived you?

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And when came you to serve our Roman captive?

Why fled you from the court? And whither?

text: Each object with a joy; the counterchange

text: Is severally in all. Let’s quit this ground,

text: And smoke the temple with our sacrifices.

text: Thou art my brother, so we’ll hold thee ever.

text: In poor beseeming; ’twas a fitment for

text: Speak, Iachimo. I had you down and might

text: Have made you finish.

speaker: IACHIMO

text: But now my heavy conscience sinks my knee,

text: As then your force did. Take that life, beseech you,

text: Which I so often owe; but your ring first,

text: And here the bracelet of the truest princess

text: That ever swore her faith.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Kneel not to me.

text: The malice towards you to forgive you. Live

text: And deal with others better.

text: Pardon’s the word to all.

speaker: POSTHUMUS

text: Call forth your soothsayer. As I slept, methought

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Here, my good lord.

speaker: LUCIUS

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Thou, Leonatus, art the lion’s whelp.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: The fit and apt construction of thy name,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Being Leo-natus, doth import so much.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text:
To
                 
                Cymbeline
                .
                 
              The piece of tender air thy virtuous daughter,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Which we call “mollis aer,” and “mollis aer”

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: We term it “mulier,” which “mulier” I divine

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Is this most constant wife; who, even now,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Answering the letter of the oracle,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text:
To

              Unknown to you, unsought, were clipped about

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: With this most tender air.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: This hath some seeming.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: The lofty cedar, royal Cymbeline,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Personates thee; and thy lopped branches point

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Thy two sons forth, who, by Belarius stol’n,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: For many years thought dead, are now revived,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: To the majestic cedar joined, whose issue

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Promises Britain peace and plenty.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Well,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: My peace we will begin. And, Caius Lucius,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Although the victor, we submit to Caesar

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And to the Roman Empire, promising

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: To pay our wonted tribute, from the which

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: We were dissuaded by our wicked queen,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Whom heavens in justice both on her and hers

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Have laid most heavy hand.

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: The fingers of the powers above do tune

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: The harmony of this peace. The vision

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Which I made known to Lucius ere the stroke

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Of this yet scarce-cold battle at this instant

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Is full accomplished. For the Roman eagle,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: From south to west on wing soaring aloft,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Lessened herself and in the beams o’ th’ sun

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: So vanished; which foreshowed our princely eagle,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Th’ imperial Caesar, should again unite

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: His favor with the radiant Cymbeline,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Which shines here in the west.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Laud we the gods,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And let our crooked smokes climb to their nostrils

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: From our blest altars. Publish we this peace

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: To all our subjects. Set we forward. Let

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A Roman and a British ensign wave

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Friendly together. So through Lud’s Town march,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And in the temple of great Jupiter

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Our peace we’ll ratify, seal it with feasts.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Set on there. Never was a war did cease,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Ere bloody hands were washed, with such a peace.
